prompt: I need a thought leadership campaign to establish us as the authority on AI-powered revenue operations for mid-market SaaS companies (200-2,000 employees, $20M-$200M ARR). Goal is top-of-funnel lead generation -- we need 400 net-new MQLs over the next quarter. Budget is $75,000 for 12 weeks. Team: two content marketers, one demand gen specialist, half-time designer. We can do webinars, LinkedIn, email nurture, and content syndication. We cannot do paid search (brand terms are too expensive in our category) or events (no budget for travel). Historical data: our benchmark report last year generated 280 MQLs at $180 CPL -- best campaign we ever ran. Blog content alone has never moved the needle. Sales team is six mid-market AEs using Salesforce with Outreach sequences. Plan the full campaign.
expectations: Pipeline generation: primary KPI is 400 net-new MQLs with an implied CPL target at or below $187.50 ($75K/400) -- the plan benchmarks against the historical $180 CPL from the benchmark report campaign and explains how channel allocation will hit or beat that efficiency

Budget efficiency: channel mix allocates $75,000 with per-channel line items, excludes paid search and events as instructed, and weights spend toward the research-driven content format that historically produced 280 MQLs -- not toward blog content which was explicitly called out as ineffective

Content leverage: lead magnet is anchored in original research (benchmark report, state-of-industry survey, or similar data-driven asset) that builds on the proven benchmark report format, with a clear plan to derive webinar topics, LinkedIn content, email nurture sequences, and syndication assets from that single research pillar

Alignment: sales-marketing integration defines a specific MQL scoring threshold that triggers Salesforce routing to the six mid-market AEs, specifies the Outreach sequence for MQL follow-up, and sets a measurable speed-to-lead SLA -- connecting marketing's 400-MQL target to AE pipeline activity

Execution velocity: content calendar fits within the capacity of two content marketers and a half-time designer without overcommitting -- production milestones are sequenced so the research asset is complete before derivative content begins, and no week exceeds feasible output

Budget efficiency: risk and contingency section includes a time-bound decision gate with a specific MQL pace threshold (e.g., below 100 MQLs by Week 4), a named metric to evaluate, and a prescribed budget reallocation from underperforming to outperforming channels -- preventing quarter-end scramble on a $75K spend
